message:
fix(trace): atomic record_event replaces split next_seq/push_event to prevent sequence interleaving

TraceBufferHandle gains a record_event(|seq| -> TraceEvent) method that
atomically allocates the next sequence number and pushes the event in a
single lock acquisition. This prevents a race where two concurrent
producers could allocate sequences N and N+1 but push events in reverse
order (N+1 before N), creating non-monotonic sequence numbers in the
trace buffer that confuse causal analysis tools.

All callers across the codebase are migrated from the two-step pattern:
  let seq = trace.next_seq();
  trace.push_event(TraceEvent::foo(seq, ...));
to:
  trace.record_event(|seq| TraceEvent::foo(seq, ...));

RuntimeState gains record_trace_event and record_task_trace_event helper
methods that encapsulate the common pattern of building a trace event
with an optional logical timestamp from the current task's Cx. The
previous attach_logical_time_for_task method is replaced by
logical_time_for_task which returns Option<LogicalTime> for use in the
closure-based recording.

Affected modules: trace/buffer, time/sleep, cx/cx, lab/runtime,
runtime/state. Every trace event emission site (timer scheduled/fired/
cancelled, user trace, chaos injection, IO ready/requested, obligation
reserve/commit/abort/leak, task spawn/complete, region created) now uses
the atomic path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
